extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint won Tour de Suisse via TT, coming within 25 meters of beating specialist EVENEPOEL for stage win to claim overall by 1:12 over GANNA.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS at 36 demonstrated ITT form so strong he nearly beat EVENEPOEL on stage, signaling Tour form peak.
KUNG showing career-best climbing without corresponding ITT breakthrough suggests specialization trade-off impacting pure TT power.
IGITA mechanical issues for EF's two-man team exposed vulnerability of skeleton squad tactics in week-long stage races.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Only 3 seconds behind EVENEPOEL in 25.6km ITT at age 36, oldest Tour de Suisse winner since Leipheimer, signals Tour form.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remko: positive: 104.85kph faster than field, 54kph average establishes him as dominant flat TT benchmark pre-Tour.
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: neutral: Climbing to career-best levels but 10 seconds down on EVENEPOEL suggests power sacrifice for weight loss.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Daniel: positive: Strong enough for top-5 TT on prototype equipment indicates dangerous Tour GC form alongside POGACAR.
rider_signals.IGITA Sergio: negative: Lost yellow jersey despite fighting mechanical issues, reveals mental fragility under equipment pressure in decisive TTs.
rider_signals.VON WILDER Elon: negative: DNF near finish suggests crash or medical issue, significant concern for Quick-Step depth.
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: negative: Mechanical in ITT while defending 4th overall for two-man EF team exposes equipment preparation gaps.
